L1.area: Data input and file parsing modules for structured formats

L2.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the XML parser processes external entity declarations without restriction. An attacker who can supply crafted XML input to the application can exploit this to read arbitrary files on the file system or perform server-side request forgery, provided the underlying XML processing library supports entity resolution.

L3.area: XML data input and parsing module for structured document extraction
L3.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the XML parser used for extracting tabular data from XML documents processes external entity declarations without restriction. When lxml is configured as the underlying XML processing library, an attacker who can supply crafted XML input can read arbitrary files on the file system in the context of the running application.

ground_truth.reason: The XML parsing function calls etree.parse() without disabling entity resolution, allowing XXE attacks when lxml is the XML backend. An attacker supplying crafted XML input can read arbitrary files from the filesystem.
